parts.answer: • Three downward arrows to show the forces of the people and the weight of the plank • Upward arrow to show reaction/contact/value force at the pivot. (1) (1)

parts.text: 11 (b) After travelling a horizontal distance of 22.0 m the ball reaches the goal. 3.00 m To score, the ball must be more than 3.00 m above the ground when it reaches the goal. Deduce whether an initial speed of 17.0 m s −1 is sufficient to score. (5) ........................................................................................................................................
parts.answer: • Use of moment = F x • Application of principle of moments • x = 0.89 m Example of calculation x = (0.9 m × 950 N − 1.1 × 250) / 650 N = 0.89 m (1) (1) (1) Total for question 11 5 Number Answer Mar k

• Use of ∆퐸퐸 grav =푚푚푚푚∆ℎ • Use of trigonometry to calculate Δh • P = 180 (W) OR • Use of W = Fs • Use of trigonometry to calculate component of weight along slope • P = 180 (W) Example of calculation: P = 72 kg × 9.81 N kg −1 × 0.51m s −1 × sin 30° = 180 W (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1)

• Use of efficiency =useful power output/total power input • Calculates useful power output • Power input = 3500 W Example of calculation 15 × 180 W / 0.78 = 3.46 × 10 W (1) (1) (1) Total for question 12 6 Number Answer Mark

• Use of v = u + 2as • Vertical component, 푢푢 푉푉 =푢푢 sin35° • Speed of ball = 17.3 (m s −1 ) Example of calculation 0 = u v – 2 × 9.81 m s −2 × 5.0 m u v = 98.1, u v = √98.1 = 9.9 m s −1 u = 9.9 / sin 35 ̊ = 17.3 m s −1 (1) (1) (1)

• Use of u H = u cos θ • Use of t = s/u H • Use of 푠푠=푢푢푢푢+ 푎푎푢푢 with u V = u sin θ and a = − g • Height = 3.2 (m) • Comparison of result consistent with calculation of height at 22 m. Example of calculation Horizontal speed = 17.0 cos 35 ̊ = 13.9 m s −1 Time to travel 22 m = 22 ÷ 13.9 = 1.58 s Initial vertical speed = 17 .0 sin 35 ̊ = 9.8 m s −1 Height gained in 1.58 s = 9.8 1.58 – 0.5  9.81 1.58 = 3.16 m (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Total for question 13 8 Number Answer Mark • Use of 휌휌= 푚푚 푉푉 • Use of 퐴퐴=π푟푟 • Use of volume in 1 second = cross section area × speed • Speed = 37.1 (m s −1 ) Example of calculation 푉푉 푢푢 = 300 kg s −1 1030 kg m −3 =0. 291 m s −1 A = π × 0.05 = 7.85 × 10 -3 m Speed = 0.291 m s −1 / 7.85 × 10 -3 m = 37.1 m s −1 (1) (1) (1) (1) • Use of p = mv • Rate of change of momentum = 1.1 × 10 kg m s −2 (ecf from (a)(i)) Example of calculation mass × speed = 300 kg 37.1 m s −1 = 1.1 1 × 10 kg m s −2 (1) (1)

• Pump applies a (forward) force to the water. • By Newton 3, water applies an (equal and) opposite/backward force to the pump Or By Newton 3, water applies a force to the pump in the opposite direction to the (flow of) water. (1) (1)

• Initially ( speed is constant because) drag force = forward force • Turning on pump gives resultant force backwards, so boat slows. • Drag force becomes less ( as boat slows) until forces balance again. (1) (1) (1) Total for question 14 11 Number Answer Mark This question assesses a student’s ability to show a coherent and logically structured answer with linkages and fully-sustained reasoning. Marks are awarded for indicative content and for how the answer is structured and shows lines of reasoning. The following table shows how the marks should be awarded for indicative content and lines of reasoning. IC points IC mark Max linkage mark available Max final mark 6 or more 4 2 6 5 3 2 5 4 3 1 4 3 2 1 3 2 2 0 2 1 1 0 1 0 0 0 0 Indicative content • In stage 1 the kinetic energy of the jumper is increasing • Because work is done by gravitational force • In stage 2 the kinetic energy of the jumper is increasing but at a decreasing rate • Because work is done on the cord (as the cord stretches) (as well as increasing k.e.) • In stage 3 t he kinetic energy of the jumper is decreasing • Because work is done on the cord at a greater rate than the gravitational force does work on the jumper Or He comes to rest because the total work done by gravitational forces is equal to work done stretching the bungee Answer shows a coherent and logical structure with linkages and fully sustained lines of reasoning demonstrated throughout. Answer is partially structured with some linkages and lines of reasoning Answer has no linkages between points and is unstructured 0 Total for question 15 6 Number Answer Mark

• Micrometer (screw gauge) Or digital (not Vernier) calliper(s) (1) 1 • Attempt to calculate gradient • Use of linear section, or tangent at origin, with use of large triangle • E = 1.2 ± 0.05 × 10 P a Example of calculation Extending straight section to 1% 120 × 10 Pa ÷ 0.01 = 1.2 × 10 Pa (1) (1) (1) • Breaking stress read from graph • Use of 퐴퐴=π푟푟 • Use of σ=F/A • F = 2.6 × 10 N Example of calculation Area = π × (2.525 × 10 −3 ) = 2.00 × 10 −5 m Force = 1 280 × 10 × 2 ×10 – 5 = 2.56 × 10 N (1) (1) (1) (1) • Use of area under graph = ½σε • Substitution of F = σA and Δx = εx • Substitution of Ax = V and ΔW = ½ FΔx Example of calculation Area = ½σε = ½ (F / A)( Δx / x ) = ½ F Δx / (A x) = ΔW / V (1) (1) (1) • Calculation of area under graph by a valid method. • Area in range 60 to 64 (MJ m −3 ) • Calculation of volume of sample • Energy = 500±20 J Example of calculation One large square = 200 × 10 × 0.01 = 2 × 10 J m –3 31 large squares Volume of sample = 0.40 m × 2.0 × 10 −5 m = 8.0 × 10 −6 m Work = 31 × 8 × 10 –6 m × 2 × 10 J m −3 = 4.96 × 10 J (1) (1) (1) (1) Total for question 16 Number Answer Mark
• Use of ∆퐹퐹=푘푘∆푥푥 • k = 1.9 (N cm −1 ) Example of calculation: k = 15 N ÷ 8 cm = 1.875 N cm −1 (1) (1)

• Use of w = mg • Use of force triangle and Pythagoras to find F Or F resolved into components • Use of trigonometry to find θ. • Use of ∆푥푥= ∆퐹퐹 푘푘 • Δx = 5.4 cm (ecf from (a), "show that" value gives 5.0 cm) • θ = 32° ( ecf from (a)) Example of calculation: θ = tan −1 (0.55 kg × 9.81 N kg −1 ÷ 8.5 N) = 32.4° Δx = √((0.55 × 9.81) + 8.5 ) ÷ 1.88 = 5.37 cm (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Total for question 17 8 Number Answer Mark
• Weight Or W, downwards • Drag Or D, downwards (1) (1)

• Use of 푉푉= π푟푟 • Use of 휌휌= 푚푚 푉푉 and W = mg • Upthrust = 3.06 × 10 −4 ( N) Example of calculation Volume of bead = 4/3 × π × (2.00 × 10 −3 m) = 3.35 × 10 −8 m Weight of displaced fluid = 930 kg m −3 × 3.35 × 10 -8 m × 9.81 N kg −1 = 3.06 × 10 −4 N (1) (1) (1) • The flow must be laminar Or There must be no turbulent flow (1) • States D = U – W • Use of F = 6πηrv • v = 0.16 (m s −1 ) • Calculate v R = 0.13 (m s −1 ) • Comparison of v with v R and correct conclusion (ecf from (b)) Alternative method of comparison of F(0.13) with D scores full marks. Example of calculation U − W =3.06 × 10 −4 − 1.05 × 10 −5 = 2.96 × 10 −4 N v = 2.96 × 10 −4 N / (6π × 4.9 × 10 −2 Pa s × 2.0 × 10 −3 m) = 1.60 × 10 -1 m s −1 v R = 1 0 × 4.9 × 10 −2 Pa s / (930 kg m −3 × 4.0 × 10 −3 m)= 1.32× 10 −1 m s −1 (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Total for question 18 11
